rulings.Case Title: GAVIN G. HANLIN ET AL VS. SOJOURN PROPERTIES INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Issues And Additional And Alternate Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT SOJOURN PROPERTIES, INC., STEVEN KING, HARCOURT GROUP LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES AND ADDITIONAL AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is continued to December 7, 2023 to be heard with other pending motions. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MINGFEI LI ET AL VS. JOHN L. YI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2023 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT COMPASS CALIFORNIA II INC., ISABELLE GROTTE DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Reading the complaint as a whole, sufficient allegations are made against the sellers' agent/broker to state each cause of action irrespective of the indemnity clause (due to independent allegations regarding failure to discharge a duty, not the seller's own failures, which are alleged to have occurred as a result of reliance on the agent's counsel. See paragraph 53). The applicability of the doctrine of unclean hands raises factual disputes that cannot be addressed on a demurrer based on the SAC as alleged=(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MINGFEI LI ET AL VS. JOHN L. YI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2023 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part, DENIED in part, and moot in part. Motion is moot as to the allegations regarding duty in light of the ruling on the demurrer. Motion is granted as to the prayer for attorney's fees without prejudice for the Cross-Complainant to seek leave to amend if and when cross-complaint may make factual allegations to support such claim. The Court notes that cross-complaint failed to cite a single case in opposition standing for the proposition that joint tortfeasors, including intentional torts, may recover fees from each other. Motion is otherwise denied. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MINGFEI LI ET AL VS. JOHN L. YI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 30, 2023 line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER TO AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is overruled at to the causes of action for indemnity as they in part arise out of negligence (a demurer cannot be sustained only to a part of a cause of action, which potentially arises out of intentional tort). Demurer is sustained with leave to amend as tot eh cause of action for negligence for the Cross-Complainant to allege all elements of this cause of action including a duty owed to the sellers, breach of the duty owed to the sellers and damage sustained due to the breach of duty owed to the sellers. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for declaratory relief to state a viable claim for actual controversy that is not otherwise addressed in this action. It appears from the opposition that the cross-complaint is seeking a determination regarding the fees and costs that will be incurred in this action, but does not cite any authority for the proposition such determination is a proper subject of a separate cause of action as opposed to the prayer for relief/part of indemnity claim, etc. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
